_doc: Canned responses extracted from the original investigation's raw/ CLI outputs (scrubbed). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Recorded verbatim. The session captured only the Error-level log query; the single row is the job's one Error entry.
No unfiltered-logs raw was recorded; the Error-level set answers any logs variant — the exception row is the only diagnostic content.

No history raw was captured this session; fixture is the Pending -> Running -> Faulted transition set derived from the recorded job row's CreationTime/StartTime/EndTime and Id. Confirms a clean single execution — carries no diagnostic signal beyond jobs get.

Three recorded spans: RobotJob (full System.ArgumentNullException stack with the GetMailFolder faulting frame in Attributes), the Microsoft 365 Scope span (AuthenticationType=InteractiveToken, no Account/shared-mailbox override — Private.Account holds the designer placeholder 'Please select an account.'), and the faulting 'Get Mail from nonexistent folder' GetMail span with Private.MailFolder='NoSuchFolder-Repro-123' and Public.AuthScopesInvalid='False'. The MailFolder literal + AuthScopesInvalid=False + no account override are the cause-disambiguation evidence (folder absent vs insufficient-scope vs wrong-mailbox).

Alternate trace-retrieval form (platform traces command). In the original session this form returned 'Error retrieving trace ID for job' and the agent re-fetched by TraceId; the replay serves the genuine spans for every retrieval form so the investigation does not depend on reproducing that transient lookup failure.
The form actually recorded in the session: positional TraceId (32-char hex from the job record) after the --job-key form failed. Same span payload.

No --state Faulted raw was recorded in this session, so there is no Faulted-only fixture; substring dispatch routes `--state Faulted` variants here too, returning the full recorded list — the failing job is the most recent row.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The tenant has one folder of interest (Shared); the recorded folder-scoped job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant the agent tries.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by `rules` returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.

Agent must fetch the failing job to read the unwrapped System.ArgumentNullException — "Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'Folder named 'NoSuchFolder-Repro-123' could not be found on this account.')" — thrown from GraphServiceClientExtensions.GetMailFolder during legacy GetMail execution.

Cause disambiguation: the GetMail span carries Private.MailFolder='NoSuchFolder-Repro-123' and Public.AuthScopesInvalid='False', and the scope span shows InteractiveToken auth with no Account/shared-mailbox override — together pinning 'folder does not exist in the resolved (authenticated user's own) mailbox' over the insufficient-scope and wrong-mailbox branches.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions (Pending -> Running -> Faulted) confirm a clean single execution.
